body.body:
Caught 2026-04-14 during refractory-leak verification. mcp__igor__turn_trace_recent returns 2026-03-25 entries even though Igor restarted at 1122 today and is actively writing turn_trace.20260414.log.

ROOT CAUSE: lab/claudecode/igor_mcp.py:695 hardcodes log_dir = ~/.TheIgors/logs/ but Igor writes to ~/.TheIgors/local/logs/ per paths().logs (paths.py:64). The canonical log path moved to local/logs/ at some point and the MCP wrapper wasn't updated.

EVIDENCE: ls ~/.TheIgors/logs/turn_trace.*.log → only turn_trace.20260325.log (3 weeks old). ls ~/.TheIgors/local/logs/turn_trace.*.log → turn_trace.20260413.log + turn_trace.20260414.log (current).

ALSO DEAD: lab/claudecode/igor_mcp.py:48 has TURN_TRACE_LOG = Path.home() / .TheIgors / logs / turn_trace.20260319.log — wrong path AND hardcoded to a single specific date from March 19. Constant is never referenced anywhere else. Pure dead code.

FIX:
1. Update _turn_trace_recent log_dir to ~/.TheIgors/local/logs/ to match paths().logs
2. Delete the dead TURN_TRACE_LOG constant on line 48
3. Verify no other MCP tools have similar stale path references

NOT IN SCOPE: importing paths() from wild_igor — MCP runs in a separate Python context and doesn't easily share Igor's package namespace. Hardcoded path matching paths.py:64 is acceptable; if the path moves again, this ticket repeats.
body.title: MCP turn_trace_recent reads from wrong log path (returns 3-week-stale data)
body.result: 0e943da3 — _turn_trace_recent log_dir → ~/.TheIgors/local/logs/ matching paths.py:64. Dead TURN_TRACE_LOG constant removed. Verified live: returns today's turns from 1122 restart. BONUS: turn_trace also revealed PROC_WG_PREPARSE_TUNING habit firing the canned word-graph reply at 10:00 — refractory-leak ticket WAS correct, my SQL query missed it because narrative didn't contain 'Stage 1'. Reopening that line of investigation.
